observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Reconstitute the Inventory Committee to spearhead the conduct of
annual physical count of all PPE, promptly prepare the Report on the
Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE), and submit the report to the Audit
Team not later than January 31st of each year.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal GSO to
reconcile their PPE records to facilitate comprehensive reports
supporting the PPE account balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count pursuant to the
provisions of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1, and furnish the
Accounting Office with RPCI for purposes of reconciling the inventory
account balances in the financial statements.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant agreed to
prepare Supplies Ledger Card for each inventory account, while the GSO
should also maintain and record the receipts and issuance of its supplies and
inventories in the corresponding Stock Cards.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and provide copy of the list
of unreleased checks to the Municipal Accountant every end of the year;
and
b. Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to restore to the Cash in
Bank and the corresponding Payable accounts the amount of unreleased
checks at year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. The Municipal Accountant to secure monthly the bank statement and
prepare the corresponding bank reconciliation statement to promptly
detect errors and make timely adjustments; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to record the bank interest income amounting
to ₱157,324.08 to reflect the correct balance of Cash in Bank-Local
Current Account.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the former
Cashier and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the amount
equivalent to the balance of the subject collections to the authorized
government depository bank as soon as possible.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for CIP account and to exert effort in tracing,
identifying and accounting of projects recorded in the CIP account; and
b. Gather the relevant documents related to the completed projects and
prepare journal entries to record the transfer from CIP account to the
appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the Subsidiary Ledgers for
Due to LGUs accounts under the General Fund and Special Education
Fund;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to send written request to the Iloilo
Provincial Government to provide detailed breakdown of the fund
transfers pertaining to Municipal and Barangay share from RPT;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding aging of
Due to NGAs and LGUs accounts and present the same in the Notes to
Financial Statements;
d. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain the implemented fund
transfers and effect full liquidation of accounts to the source agencies, and
draw necessary journal entry thereof;
e. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refund the unused fund transfer
balances with identified source agencies, or seek their written approval
that the balance shall be utilized for other purposes; and
f. Direct the concerned Municipal Officials to implement the pending
projects under the trust fund for the purpose they are intended to.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the concerned
departments to submit the RIS to the General Service Officer who will then
consolidate using the SSMI, and be submitted to the Municipal Accountant as
basis for the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to record the cost of
supplies consumed during the period and the corresponding ending
inventory.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to consider establishing
centralized storage facility to properly account for and safeguard the supplies.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to consider establishing
centralized storage facility to properly account for and safeguard the supplies.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the concerned
department/end-user and the BAC to prepare the PPMP and APP in
accordance with the prescribed template of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Bids and
Awards Committee to ensure that offers submitted by prospective suppliers
are complete with technical specifications, including specific brand names
and model or variants, in their quotations and other procurement
documents.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
concerned personnel to properly accomplish the Purchase Order
containing complete technical specifications including brand name, model,
accessories, and terms and date of delivery.

We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that, in
preparing the Purchase Request, references to brand names should be
avoided. Instead, the specifications for the procurement of goods should be
based on relevant characteristics, functionality, and performance
requirements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal
Engineer, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
determine whether the contractor has committed any of the grounds for
imposition of penalty and issuance of blacklisting order, and to notify the
contractor concerned in writing; and

The Municipal Mayor upon recommendation of the BAC, issue a Blacklisting
Order to disqualify the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of
all government projects in accordance with Section 69.1 of the Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184, and provisions of GPPB Uniform Guidelines for Blacklisting
of Manufacturers, Suppliers, Distributors, Contractors and Consultants.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Treasurer to intensify efforts in the enforcement of collections for unpaid
market rental fees. Likewise, to develop and implement appropriate plans,
policies, and strategies to motivate prompt payment by stallholders, thereby
improving revenue generation from market operations, ultimately enhancing
the Municipality’s overall income from this source.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the MDRRMO
and Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 5.1.5
of COA Circular No. 2012-002, specifically in the submission of monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF so that proper utilization,
recording, and release of the fund could be timely verified, and any
rectifications to the proper charging and recording thereof can immediately
effected.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
LDRRMO to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the
PPAs chargeable against the unexpended balance of LDDRMF under the
Special Trust Fund, and ensure that additional PPAs are supported by
supplemental budget for the purpose to assure validity of disbursements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Municipal Officials concerned and the GAD Focal Point System to prepare
the Municipality’s GAD Agenda for the three-year period, as required under
Item C2 par. 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and Item 5
par. 6 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 to establish the
Municipality’s strategic framework and plan on gender mainstreaming,
women’s empowerment, and gender equality which should be the basis in the
preparation of the annual GPB.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate adjusting entries recognizing the major replacement of the evacuation center as an asset account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fast track reconciliation of the subject depreciable PPE accounts and provide the corresponding depreciation in accordance with the guidelines of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide necessary details of assets in the depreciation schedule, and compute for appropriate allowance for depreciation for the depreciable components of the Road Networks in compliance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator prepare the LDRRMFIP aligned with the NDRRMP as provided by NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Officials to ensure that allocation on Confidential Fund is duly supported with documentary requirements consistent with the Joint Circular No. 2015-01, and designate a Special Disbursing Officer to handle disbursements relative thereto.
We recommended that the BAC ensure timely posting of NOA on the PhilGEPS and other relevant information are correctly stated thereon.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the subject documentary requirements; and b. Acting General Services Officer, the Municipal Accountant, and the Chairperson, Bids and Awards Committee ensure completeness of supporting documents before payment to the contractors in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 so that propriety and validity of disbursements are assured.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause submission of the enumerated DVs and their supporting documents to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspensions; and closely monitor compliance on the prompt submission of accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursement vouchers and supporting documents submitted to the Audit Team bears the written signatures of approving officials.

We likewise recommended that the Municipality re-evaluate the provision of rice allowance on a regular basis to Mt. Tigers Rescue Volunteers in the sense that the same are non-compulsory and non-obligatory in so far as volunteering is generally considered an altruistic and humanitarian activity which the said volunteer group provides for no financial gain or benefit therefrom. However, such allowance would be necessary to them during periods with higher disaster expectancy, such as floods and typhoons during rainy season and during periods of imminent danger, onset of calamities and actual rescue operations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Chairperson and Members of Early Childhood Care and Development Council (ECCDC) program and allocate to Special Education Fund (SEF) and Gender and Development (GAD) fund the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) intended for the said purpose.

We further recommended that the ECCDC adhere to Section 7, Rule VII of the IRR of RA No. 10410 and Items 3.4 and 4.1.7 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s.2017, dated January 19, 2017, to ensure optimum attainment of the purpose, goals and objectives of the ECCD program.

We recommended that the Chairperson and Members of the LSB formulate a three-year program to be integrated in the LGU’s CDP and LDIP prior to the formulation and preparation of its Annual Budget for the incoming year.

 We further recommended that the OIC - Municipal Treasurer and OIC - Municipal Accountant submit to the LSB certified statement containing the following: 1) actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year; (2) actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year; and (3) the estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year not later than July 15 of the current year.

We furthermore recommended that the LSB furnish the OIC - Municipal Treasurer and OIC - Municipal Accountant a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or certification of availability and for recording purposes not later that seven (7) days after the approval of the SEF Budget.
We likewise recommended that the LSB submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF Utilization, as prepared by the OIC - Municipal Accountant to the DepEd CO through appropriate channels, copy furnished Local Sanggunian, DBM Regional Offices and DILG not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter for quarterly reports and not later than February 15 of the following year for annual reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the PRAISE Committee to submit the above-mentioned required information and documents on the grant of rice as non-monetary award to the municipal employees and officials.

We further recommendedthat the LGU refrain from granting PRAISE awards to all municipal officials and employees without adhering to principles and rules of procedures of the PRAISE system pursuant tothe forgoing guidelines.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Local Development Council, in coordination with Municipal Budget Officer, direct the said council to strictly comply with the guidelines set forth in the JMC as far as appropriation and application of the 20% Development Fund are concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer III to assess the adequacy and availability of basic rescue and response equipment in the LDRRM Office and prioritize the procurement of early warning systems, search, rescue and retrieval equipment and other equipage which are efficient, effective and long-term-purpose tools necessary for disaster preparedness and disaster response, rather than intensifying appropriations and expenditures in favor of MOOE. It is also advised that measures should be employed on how to minimize the MOOE spending in favor of capital expenditures that would result to long-term benefits. 

We furthermore recommended that the Municipality through the Sangguniang Bayan consider the passing of an ordinance authorizing the collection of ambulance fee to defray the expenses charged to LDRRMF that will be used for the repair and maintenance, payment of wages of job order personnels for the operation of 24/7 Operation Center, purchase of cylinder flashtank oxygen, fuel, oil and lubricants consumption.

We also recommended that the LDRRM Officer III properly program the LDRRMFIP for efficient and effective utilization of LDRRMF in conformity with Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and Item A and B of DILG MC No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.

We likewise recommended that the Municipality re-evaluate the provision of rice allowance on a regular basis to Mt. Tigers Rescue Volunteers in the sense that the same are non-compulsory and non-obligatory in so far as volunteering is generally considered an altruistic and humanitarian activity which the said volunteer group provides for no financial gain or benefit therefrom. However, such allowance would be necessary to them during periods with higher disaster expectancy, such as floods and typhoons during rainy season and during periods of imminent danger, onset of calamities and actual rescue operations.

We finally recommended that the Municipality separately appropriate funds for the Solid Waste Management Program and related activities from the LDRRMF, and rather establish the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to supervise the programs, projects and activities in accordance with Republic Act No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to submit within the reglementary period copies of pertinent contracts and documents as enumerated above together with the other specific requirements as mentioned in the annexes of the said circular for technical review/inspection thereof. 

We also recommended that all government projects, programs and activities (completed, on-going and to be implemented) should be included in the quarterly report for submission to the office of the auditor.

We further recommended that the Municipality adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 for a timely evaluation of the programs, projects and activities to avoid suspensions in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as LSB Chairperson direct the Local School Board, in coordination with Municipal Budget Officer, to prioritize in the budget preparation the priority programs, projects and activities chargeable against SEF pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s.2017 dated January 19, 2017.

We also recommendedthat the Municipal Mayor through the Sangguniang Bayan conduct periodic review of the operations of LEEs and evaluate the profitability and self-sufficiency of the individual LEEs. On DPWH, DA, NAPC, BRIA and NHA fund transfers – 
1.	The Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to communicate and inform the source agencies of the existing unutilized balance, and review the MOA for the said fund transfer. On DENR fund transfers – 
2.	The Municipal Mayor direct the department head concerned to review the MOA for the said fund transfer and the schedule of its implementation. If there are agreements indicated, seek written approval from the DENR for extension of the implementation.
3.	For the department head concerned, to hasten the full implementation and maximum utilization of the fund considering that the fund was transferred a couple of years ago.
4.	In case the DENR desires to have these financial assistance returned due to non-implementation, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer immediately cause the return of the fund.

On DOT fund transfer

6.	For the department head concerned, to formulate the municipality’s action  plan to implement the said program in line with its tourism activities and pursue the same.

On the BFP fund transfer –
The Municipal Mayor direct the Officer-in-Charge of the local fire station to formulate the municipality’s action plan to utilize the said fund for inclusion in the annual budget and procurement plan of the municipality.

On the various unutilized fund transfers –
8.	The Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to identify the composition of the accumulated unutilized balances and seek written approval with their source agencies for the proper disposition of the funds consonant with the above-mentioned regulations.

We therefore recommended that the Municipality return or remit the unutilized balance of Fund Transfers totaling P1,601,175.80 to the Bureau of the Treasury pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision dated November 19, 2013, or to its source agency pursuant to Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) create Disposal and Appraisal Committee to spearhead and expedite the divestment/disposal of unserviceable properties in consonance with existing laws, rules and regulations so as to prevent their further deterioration and to generate income therefrom.

Municipal Accountant to take up immediately all valid reconciling items requiring adjustments in the books including the reversion of the amount of stale checks and the setting up of the corresponding payable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections to the authorized government depository bank intact daily and to strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference of ₱13,869.32 between the cash books with that of the accounting records to ensure reliability of account balance as presented in the financial statements.

Municipal Engineer to furnish the MTO copy of the actual area of the stalls as reference for computation of rates based on the municipal revenue code.

Properly program the LDRRMFIP for efficient and effective utilization of LDRRMF in conformity with Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and Item A and B of DILG MC No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.

Concerned officials and employees to stop the practice of paying through cash advances when procuring goods and other services for the implementation of municipal programs, projects, and activities. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare the consolidated Program, Projects and Activities (PPAs) that have direct linkages in addressing gender issues pursuant to PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, prior to the allocation of GAD budget by the Municipal Budget Office to ensure effectiveness on the fund’s utilization.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances whose purpose has already been served and strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

On DPWH, DA, NAPC, BRIA and NHA fund transfers –

On DOT fund transfer

observations.recommendation:

Tigers Rescue Volunteers in the sense that the same are non-compulsory and non-obligatory in so far as volunteering is generally considered an altruistic and humanitarian activity that the said volunteer group provides for no financial gain or benefit therefrom. Such allowance would be provided as support during periods with higher disaster expectancy, such as floods and typhoons during rainy season and during periods of imminent danger, onset of calamities and actual rescue operations.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to confer and direct the concerned offices to institute/instill adequate internal control over cash to safeguard government funds and minimize the risk of possible losses/misappropriations consonant to the above-cited regulations. The Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to communicate and inform the source agencies of the existing unutilized balance, and review the MOA for the said fund transfer. In case, the source agencies intends to have these funds returned, the Municipal Mayor direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the return of the fund; otherwise, seek written approval so that it may be transferred to the general fund to be utilized for another purpose.
On DENR fund transfers –
The Municipal Mayor direct the department head concerned to review the MOA for the said fund transfer and the schedule of its implementation. If there are agreements indicated, seek written approval from the DENR for extension of the implementation.
For the department head concerned, to hasten the full implementation and maximum utilization of the fund considering that the fund was transferred a couple of years ago.
In case the DENR desires to have these financial assistance returned due to non-implementation, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer immediately cause the return of the fund.
On DOT fund transfer –
The Municipal Mayor direct the department head concerned to inquire the real intent of the said financial subsidy or the conditions agreed upon in the transfer of the fund from the Department of Tourism.
For the department head concerned, to formulate the municipality’s action  plan to implement the said program in line with its tourism activities and pursue the same.
On the BFP fund transfer –
The Municipal Mayor direct the Officer-in-Charge of the local fire station to formulate the municipality’s action plan to utilize the said fund for inclusion in the annual budget and procurement plan of the municipality.
On the various unutilized fund transfers –
The Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to identify the composition of the accumulated unutilized balances and seek written approval with their source agencies for the proper disposition of the funds consonant with the above-mentioned regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the following courses of action:
Require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.
Instruct the General Services/ Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the property stock cards and ledger cards, respectively and reconcile them with other property records like Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) in accordance with Section 120 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through an executive order assign one or two personnel, as deemed necessary, to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption (MRFC) duly supported by Monthly Reports of Official to enable the Auditor to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumption. Consequently, MRFC should be regularly prepared and submitted as required.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements monthly and submit the same to the Auditor within the prescribed period for verification pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011, dated October 2, 1996, to ensure the accuracy of Cash in Bank balances. Timely preparation and verification of the financial reports will guide management in making sound decisions regarding the financial position of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the assigned department head/s or personnel to fast track the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI), attached as “Annex IV” of the CY 2015 Annual Audit Report (attached herewith for ready reference), to the auditor concerned to facilitate monitoring and validations of the actions taken by the LGU on the related audit observations and recommendations, and eventually to submit a validation report to the Regional Director, COA Region VI, for monitoring purpose. Compliance on the submission of the same report to the above-mentioned oversight bodies should be made.

Moreover, compliance is necessary to avoid further penalties as provided in this act.

We recommended that the municipality align itself with Article 77 of R.A. 7160 and also revisit Section 3.0 of COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012, Annex B of COA Circular 2012-003 Example No. 3.1 and Section (c) of Article 170 of the IRR of R.A. 7160 to be guided with the guidelines regarding the grant of benefits and incentives to officials and employees. Moreover, CSC MC No.  No. 1, S. 2001 provides guidelines for the PRAISE system of recognizing and rewarding the municipality’s officials and employees for their exemplary accomplishments and efforts
We further recommended the refund of the amount disbursed for the service award rings amounting to P41,141.25 by the concerned officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials hire qualified personnel to occupy the LDRRM Officer - Designate and three (3) mandatory staff position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2, of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.3 and 5.4 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer - Designate follow the four (4) thematic areas and programs/projects/activities in accordance to Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 as reflected in the NDRRMP in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the LDRRM Officer - Designate include in the LDRRMFIP of the municipality the programs/projects/activities of Quick Response Fund in the LDRRMFIP in accordance in accordance to Section 4.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013. The plan should further include PPAs for reconstruction and recovery.

We recommended that the LDRRMCs convene and hold regular meetings once every three months on dates and/or special meetings as maybe deemed necessary in accordance to Rule 5, Section 5, of IRR of RA No. 10121.

We recommended that the LDRRMO adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the audit team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and the Local Development Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRM Officer - Designate to assess the adequacy and availability of basic rescue and response equipment in the LDRRMO and prioritize the procurement of early warning systems, search, rescue and retrieval equipment and other equipage which are efficient, effective and long-term-purpose tools necessary for disaster preparedness and disaster response, rather than intensifying appropriations and expenditures in favor of MOOE. We likewise recommend that the LDRRMC find measures on how to minimize the spending in MOOE but prioritize the expenditures to capital outlay that would result to long-term benefits.
We also recommended that the municipality through the Sangguniang Bayan consider the passing of an ordinance authorizing the collection of ambulance fee to defray the expenses charged to LDRRMF that will be used for the repair and maintenance, payment of wages of job order personnel for the operation of 24/7 Operation Center, purchase of cylinder flash tank oxygen and fuel, oil and lubricants consumption.
We further recommended that the LDRRM Officer - Designate properly program the LDRRMFIP for efficient and effective utilization of LDRRMF in conformity with Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012.
We furthermore recommended that the municipality re-evaluate the provision of rice allowance to Mt. Tigers Rescue Volunteers in the sense that the same are non-compulsory and non-obligatory in so far as volunteering is generally considered an altruistic and humanitarian activity that the said volunteer group provides for no financial gain or benefit therefrom. Such allowance would be provided as support during periods with higher disaster expectancy, such as floods and typhoons during rainy season and during periods of imminent danger, onset of calamities and actual rescue operations.
Finally, we recommended that the municipality separately appropriate funds for the Solid Waste Management Program and related activities from the LDRRMF, and rather establish the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to supervise the programs, projects and activities in accordance with Republic Act No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Local School Board to discontinue the practice of appropriating and disbursing administrative supplies and I.T equipment against the Special Education Fund.
We also recommended that the Chairperson, Municipal Local School Board  prioritize in the budget preparation the expenses chargeable to Special Education Fund in accordance with the above-cited implementing, laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson and Members adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 on all procurement of the municipality and ensure that the supporting documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC -Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate their cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reorganize the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to ensure the adoption of a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program. A regular meeting shall be made to keep abreast of the activities embodied on their plan so that necessary actions be effected in areas needing improvement and to ensure that the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan will be implemented accordingly and responsibly.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, formulate and adopt revenue generating measures to ensure the viability of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan such as, but not limited to:
Strengthening the collection of fines and penalties to the households and institutions who violates the said act.
Recycling of junk materials and scraps that could be a source of livelihood.
Promoting organic fertilizers and sale from the vermi-compost to the farmers and   other institutions.
Enactment of an ordinance that includes residential areas in the collection of garbage fees.
We also recommended that the LGU should purchase its own lot for a comprehensive disposal facility following the guidelines prescribed under R.A 9003.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create and establish the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office as prescribed by RA 10121, dated May 27, 2010 or the PDRRM Act of 2010 and NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
We also recommend that staffing requirements be properly complied with according to the aforementioned guidelines and provisions.
